commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=08db0d141479bfe48fd0a992a04900bee9c51d61)
commands.review_summary:
The PR deletes the `replayMetadata` side-effect guard from `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` and adjusts incomplete-turn tests to allow silent-error retry after prior session side effects.

PR surface: Source -4, Tests +31. Total +27 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main still blocks the linked empty provider-error retry when accumulated replay metadata reports side effects, and this PR's unsafe-retry regression is visible because `replaySafe:false` tool metadata is not covered by `hasAttemptTerminalState`. I did not run a live OpenRouter 5xx reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action and maintainer review are needed because the branch has a blocking retry-safety defect and the external real-behavior proof gate cannot be satisfied by repair automation.

target.title: fix(agents): retry transient silent errors after prior session side effects
target.body:
Closes #97877

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where agents that had run at least one tool call earlier in a session would silently terminate with no user-visible reply and no retry when a downstream provider returned a transient HTTP 5xx (or any `stopReason="error"` turn with empty output) on a later turn. The runtime's documented `[empty-error-retry]` resubmit path (up to `MAX_EMPTY_ERROR_RETRIES = 3`) was never reached.

## Why This Change Was Made

`shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` rejected retry whenever `attempt.replayMetadata.hadPotentialSideEffects` was set. That flag is produced by `mergeEmbeddedRunReplayState`, which OR-merges monotonically across the whole session — once any prior turn ran a tool, the flag stays true for every subsequent turn. So a clean thinking-only or empty-content error turn later in the session was treated as terminal even though the failed turn itself executed no tools.

The retry path resubmits the same prompt; already-committed prior tool results remain in transcript history as context and are not re-executed. There is no in-flight tool activity to protect against on a turn that produced `stopReason="error"` with no content.

The fix removes the session-wide `hadPotentialSideEffects` guard from `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn`. Current-turn tool activity is still detected by the existing `hasAttemptTerminalState` check (`clientToolCalls`, `yieldDetected`, async tool activity, `successfulCronAdds`, committed messaging-tool delivery, accepted session spawns, etc.), which is per-turn scoped and is the correct signal for "this turn already committed side effects." The `replayMetadata` field is dropped from the function's `attempt` Pick since it is no longer consulted.

This matches the fix shape proposed during triage: distinguish "this turn's tool calls" from "this session's accumulated tool history" and apply the guard only to the former.

## User Impact

Agents bound to providers that intermittently return 5xx (OpenRouter and similar) now recover from transient errors mid-session instead of going idle silently. Users receive either the recovered response or, after retries are exhausted, the existing `⚠️ Agent couldn't generate a response…` warning. Sessions with tool activity earlier in the conversation are no longer permanently blocked from retrying clean error turns.

## Evidence

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts`: removed the `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(...).hadPotentialSideEffects` guard from `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn`; `hasAttemptTerminalState` already gates on per-turn tool activity.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`:
  - Updated "does not retry errored thinking-only turns when the current turn had tool activity" to use a current-turn `clientToolCalls` entry (per-turn scope), preserving the no-retry semantics for in-flight tool activity.
  - Added "retries errored thinking-only turns when prior session turns had side effects but the current turn is clean (#97877)" as a direct regression test for the bug scenario — `replayMetadata.hadPotentialSideEffects: true` with no per-turn signals now returns `true`.
- Local: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts` → 112/112 passed (6.01s).
- Local: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental` → clean.
